spec_match.rationale: Accurately validates key RWS companion points like requestStorageAccessFor, requestStorageAccess, and storage permissions.

interactive.rationale: Runs an automated capability probe on page load, updating the DOM with formatted tables and custom color badges.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the parent index description of checking RWS feature existence in the browser.

edge_cases.rationale: Protects checks inside try-catch boundaries to handle missing browser properties securely.

references.rationale: Includes clean breadcrumbs and the mandatory ChromeStatus feature entry reference in the footer.

design_system.rationale: Fully complies with design guidelines using standard fonts, card headers, and color mixes.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

openQuestions.title: Add manual 'Re-run Probe' button
openQuestions.detail: While the page runs automatically on load, adding a manual refresh/re-run action button would make the interface feel more active and allow users to re-evaluate state after tweaking browser flags.

summary: A robust, clean live API probe verifying RWS compatibility vectors dynamically. Protected by try-catch boundaries and completely styled according to the project design variables.
